behavioral_changes.summary: Cross-lakehouse catalog validation now classifies a Guid.Empty lakehouse ObjectId as corrupted metadata and throws CatalogException with MLV_DATA_CORRUPTED instead of a generic server error.
PySpark DAG construction now treats a missing SourceInfo payload as corrupted catalog data and throws CatalogException with MLV_DATA_CORRUPTED.
PySpark DAG construction now treats a structurally invalid/null-parsed SourceInfo object as corrupted catalog data and throws CatalogException with MLV_DATA_CORRUPTED.
PySpark DAG construction now converts SourceInfo deserialization/parsing failures into CatalogException with MLV_DATA_CORRUPTED.
PySpark DAG construction now treats missing workspaceId or notebookId fields in SourceInfo as corrupted catalog data.
PySpark DAG construction now treats invalid GUID strings in SourceInfo as corrupted catalog data.
Catalog table rebuilding now classifies an empty table name as MLV_DATA_CORRUPTED instead of throwing ArgumentException.
Catalog table rebuilding now classifies an empty table type as MLV_DATA_CORRUPTED instead of throwing ArgumentException.
Data quality report creation now classifies null lakehouse properties as CatalogException with MLV_DATA_CORRUPTED.
The data-quality-report controller pipeline now catches CatalogException and returns the exception's HTTP status and message to the client.

Selected-MLV validation in the public execution-definition API now uses ErrorRegistry-based MLV_SELECTED_NOT_FOUND responses that list missing MLV names, replacing the old hardcoded MLV_ENTITY_NOT_FOUND text.
A new MLV_DATA_CORRUPTED error code and registry template were added, with recreate/retry/support guidance and FullyQualifiedMLVName substitution.
The MLV_ENTITY_NOT_FOUND registry message was repurposed to describe incomplete source metadata for a materialized lake view, using MLVName substitution.

grounding_evidence.reason: Changed the invalid-ObjectId branch from a generic server error to a specific corrupted-metadata CatalogException with formatted error text.
New defensive guard for null lakehouse properties now raises a typed CatalogException carrying the corrupted-data code and message.
Adds a new controller branch that preserves CatalogException status/message instead of falling through to broader handlers.
Switches invalid-selected-MLV validation from hardcoded text to a standardized error code/message path that injects the missing names list.
Replaces the PySpark missing-SourceInfo InvalidOperationException with a specific CatalogException/MLV_DATA_CORRUPTED path.
Changes the branch for invalid/null-parsed SourceInfo objects to the new corrupted-metadata error contract.
Normalizes exceptions raised while interpreting SourceInfo into CatalogException with the corrupted-data code, preserving the inner exception.
Adds a dedicated corruption classification when required SourceInfo fields are blank or whitespace.
Changes the invalid-GUID error path for PySpark source metadata to the new CatalogException/MLV_DATA_CORRUPTED contract.
Replaces the empty-table-name ArgumentException with a typed corrupted-metadata CatalogException.
Replaces the empty-table-type ArgumentException with a typed corrupted-metadata CatalogException.
Introduces the new error-code contract and its documented meaning.

grounding_evidence.excerpt:
// Data integrity / corrupted metadata error codes
{ ErrorCode.MLV_DATA_CORRUPTED, $"Catalog data does not have information needed to process this request. Recreate the materialized lake view '{{{Constants.FullyQualifiedMLVNameKey}}}' and retry the operation. If issue persists, reach out to support." },
grounding_evidence.reason: Defines the user-visible message template for the new MLV_DATA_CORRUPTED code, including placeholder substitution and recovery guidance.

grounding_evidence.excerpt: { ErrorCode.MLV_ENTITY_NOT_FOUND, $"The materialized lake view '{{{Constants.MLVNameKey}}}' has incomplete source metadata and can't be processed. Update the lake view definition to include a valid source, then try again." },
grounding_evidence.reason: Repurposes MLV_ENTITY_NOT_FOUND to a new incomplete-source-metadata message contract with MLV-name substitution.

scenario_sketches.title: Guid.Empty lakehouse ObjectId is reported as MLV_DATA_CORRUPTED
scenario_sketches.rationale: Exercise the cross-lakehouse metadata branch where ObjectId is Guid.Empty and assert CatalogException carries HTTP 500, code MLV_DATA_CORRUPTED, and a message naming the lakehouse/workspace descriptor.

scenario_sketches.title: PySpark MLVs missing SourceInfo fail with CatalogException/MLV_DATA_CORRUPTED
scenario_sketches.rationale: Cover the PySpark-only guard where SourceInfo is absent and verify the flow no longer throws InvalidOperationException but instead returns the standardized corrupted-data CatalogException.

scenario_sketches.title: PySpark MLVs with null or invalid parsed SourceInfo fail as corrupted metadata
scenario_sketches.rationale: Use a SourceInfo payload that reaches the 'invalid source info' branch and assert the exception type/code/status are CatalogException + MLV_DATA_CORRUPTED + 500.

scenario_sketches.title: PySpark SourceInfo deserialization failures surface as MLV_DATA_CORRUPTED
scenario_sketches.rationale: Pass malformed SourceInfo that triggers parsing/deserialization failure and verify the catch path wraps it in CatalogException with the corrupted-data code while preserving the inner exception.

scenario_sketches.title: Blank workspaceId or notebookId in PySpark SourceInfo is classified as MLV_DATA_CORRUPTED
scenario_sketches.rationale: Probe valid, empty, and whitespace values for required SourceInfo fields and assert blank/whitespace inputs now hit the dedicated corrupted-metadata error path.

scenario_sketches.title: Invalid GUID strings in PySpark SourceInfo are classified as MLV_DATA_CORRUPTED
scenario_sketches.rationale: Use non-GUID workspace/notebook identifiers after structural parsing succeeds and verify the invalid-GUID branch now throws CatalogException with MLV_DATA_CORRUPTED.

scenario_sketches.title: Empty catalog table name is surfaced as MLV_DATA_CORRUPTED
scenario_sketches.rationale: Exercise catalog-table rebuild with empty versus populated names and assert the empty-name case now produces CatalogException/500/MLV_DATA_CORRUPTED instead of ArgumentException.

scenario_sketches.title: Empty catalog table type is surfaced as MLV_DATA_CORRUPTED
scenario_sketches.rationale: Exercise catalog-table rebuild with empty versus populated table types and assert the empty-type case now produces CatalogException/500/MLV_DATA_CORRUPTED instead of ArgumentException.

scenario_sketches.title: Null lakehouse properties in data-quality creation surface MLV_DATA_CORRUPTED
scenario_sketches.rationale: Make GetLakehouseDetailsAsync return a LakehouseDetails object with null Properties and verify handler classification changes from InvalidOperationException to CatalogException/MLV_DATA_CORRUPTED.

scenario_sketches.title: CreateDataQualityReportAsync preserves CatalogException status and body
scenario_sketches.rationale: Have the data-quality handler throw a CatalogException and assert the controller returns the same HTTP status and includes the exception message in the response body.

scenario_sketches.title: Selected-MLV validation returns MLV_SELECTED_NOT_FOUND with missing names
scenario_sketches.rationale: Cover one-missing and multiple-missing selected-MLV inputs and assert the 400 response is driven by ErrorRegistry MLV_SELECTED_NOT_FOUND text, includes the missing names, and no longer uses the old MLV_ENTITY_NOT_FOUND phrasing.

scenario_sketches.title: ErrorRegistry formats MLV_DATA_CORRUPTED with recreate and support guidance
scenario_sketches.rationale: Unit-test direct ErrorRegistry formatting for MLV_DATA_CORRUPTED with a FullyQualifiedMLVName parameter, including standard-prefix output and the recreate/retry/support guidance.

scenario_sketches.title: ErrorRegistry formats MLV_ENTITY_NOT_FOUND as incomplete-source-metadata guidance
scenario_sketches.rationale: Unit-test direct ErrorRegistry formatting for MLV_ENTITY_NOT_FOUND with an MLVName parameter and assert the message now describes incomplete source metadata instead of inaccessible/deleted entities.
